medianHouseholdIncome.label: Median household income
medianHouseholdIncome.note: 1st of Tennessee's 95 counties; ~167% of the U.S. median.

medianHomeValue.label: Median home value
medianHomeValue.note: Roughly 2.2x the national median owner-occupied home value.

metrics.label: Bridges in poor condition
metrics.note: Modeled below the TN statewide poor rate (~4.4%); Williamson's ~295 bridges are relatively new and well-funded.

metrics.label: Roads in poor ride quality
metrics.note: Modeled; well-maintained arterials but rapid growth strains the network. TN statewide is moderate.

metrics.label: Roundabouts vs. signalized intersections
metrics.note: Field survey: a 'stoplight city' with very few roundabouts; near the weak anchor.

metrics.label: Traffic deaths
metrics.note: Modeled well below the TN statewide rate (~17); Williamson is consistently the healthiest/safest county in the state.

metrics.label: Sidewalk coverage
metrics.note: Field survey: underdeveloped sidewalks; newer upscale subdivisions built without them; multi-use paths on some main roads but with many gaps.

metrics.label: Park acres per 1,000 residents
metrics.source: Trust for Public Land / local parks dept
metrics.note: Modeled from ~34 county park properties (incl. the ~800-acre SW Williamson Regional Park) plus municipal parks; acreage exists but is dispersed.

metrics.label: Residents within a 10-min walk of a park
metrics.note: Modeled low: sprawling subdivisions and large-lot development put most parkland beyond a walk despite substantial acreage.

metrics.label: Playground quality
metrics.note: Field survey: Franklin playgrounds dated (1990s-2000s), mostly mulch surfaces (only one rubberized), peeling paint. Brentwood's are somewhat nicer. Wealth flows to private, not public, play space.

metrics.label: Library visits per capita
metrics.note: ~1,780.8 visits per 1,000 in legal service area; #6 of 185 TN systems. A genuine public-realm bright spot.

metrics.label: Tree canopy
metrics.note: Modeled from the wooded rolling countryside of the Nashville Basin/Highland Rim; lower in the densely developed northern suburbs.

metrics.label: Learning rate
metrics.note: Modeled above 1.0: WCS is Tennessee's #1 district (67% math / 69% reading proficiency vs. ~34%/37% statewide), consistent with above-average learning growth.

metrics.label: Grade levels vs. national average
metrics.note: Modeled: one of the highest-achieving districts nationally; students test well above the national mean.

metrics.label: Bachelor's degree or higher (age 25+)
metrics.note: 62.5% (2023 ACS); ~64% in 2024 estimates vs. ~32% statewide.

metrics.label: Life expectancy
metrics.note: Longest life expectancy of any TN county; ~7 years above the state average.

metrics.label: Child wellbeing index
metrics.source: modeled from CDC PLACES / KIDS COUNT
metrics.note: Modeled high: very low child poverty, top health outcomes, and top-ranked schools statewide.

metrics.label: Violent crime
metrics.source: FBI Crime Data Explorer / TBI TIBRS
metrics.note: Modeled from TBI/FBI data; among the lowest violent-crime counties in Tennessee (third-party estimates run ~3.3/1k; official incident-based rate is lower).

metrics.label: Drinking-water violation severity
metrics.note: Modeled low: major districts (Milcrofton, H.B.&T.S.) show clean current MCL compliance; Nolensville/College Grove had a minor recent coliform hit. Historical cumulative counts exist but current health-based compliance is good.

metrics.label: Broadband availability (100/20 Mbps)
metrics.note: ~100% of residents have access at 100/20 Mbps.

metrics.label: Gigabit availability
metrics.note: ~74% of residents have gigabit (1000/100 Mbps) access.

metrics.label: National Register listings per 10,000
metrics.note: 132 NRHP listings (incl. 2 National Historic Landmarks) / ~264k residents; deep Civil War and antebellum history around Franklin.

metrics.label: Cultural venues per 10,000
metrics.note: Modeled from Franklin's museums, historic sites, the Franklin Theatre, and Factory at Franklin venues; concentrated in the county seat.

metrics.label: Walkable town center
metrics.note: Field survey: downtown Franklin is genuinely excellent (1800s stock, tasteful, Harpeth Hotel) but a tourist rather than living downtown, with little walkable residential. Brentwood has no town center at all (pure sprawl). High quality, but the exception county-wide.

metrics.label: Annual capital outlay per resident
metrics.note: Modeled: heavy recent capital spending is driven almost entirely by school construction for growth, not public-realm streetscape/parks investment.

metrics.label: Net capital assets per resident
metrics.note: Modeled: county-government-only governmental capital assets were ~$281M in FY20 (~$1.1k/capita); adding the large WCS school-plant assets brings the combined figure to a modeled ~$4.5k/capita. Modest relative to the county's private wealth.

metrics.label: Active-investment trend
metrics.note: Field/assessed: real investment is happening but concentrated in schools and roads keeping pace with growth; the walkable public realm (sidewalks, roundabouts, playgrounds) lags the times.
